text: Age-predicted maximal heart rate is more accurately estimated by the equation 208 - 0.7 × age than the traditional 220 - age formula.
Age alone is the primary predictor of maximal heart rate, with a strong inverse correlation (r = -0.90).
Maximal heart rate does not differ between men and women after accounting for age.
Habitual physical activity level does not significantly influence age-predicted maximal heart rate.
The traditional 220 - age formula underestimates maximal heart rate in older adults, leading to underestimation of exercise intensity during testing and inappropriate exercise prescription.
Heart rate training zones calculated from the 220 - age formula may be set too low for older adults, resulting in insufficient training stimulus.
